[[ a J» ec] df e [ ff |
dal nucle | al cell d apparat
1 us
IE EET le
dal nucle | yo d apparat
1 sac us
dal nucle yo apparat | d
1 sac us
= ETE EE
dal al nucle apparat | d
cell i us
Page No.-26, Easy
2.2.3 Pollination
46. Pollination is-
A) transfer of motile pollen to stigma
B) transfer of non-motile anther to stigma
C) transfer of motile anther to stigma
D) transfer of non-motile pollen to stigma
Page No.-27, Easy
47. Transfer of pollen from anther to stigma of
same flower is called-
A) Autogamy B) Geitonogany
C) Xenogeny D) None of these
Page No.-27, Easy
48. Read the given statements-
(i) Autogamy cannot occur in open flower.
(ii) Geitonogany cannot occur in closed
flower.
Choose the appropriate answer-
A) (i) is correct but (ii) is wrong
B) (i) is wrong but (ii) is correct
C) (i) and (ii) are both correct
D) (i) and (ii) are both wrong
Page No.-28, Easy
49. Complete autogamy is rare in-
A) Closed flower
B) Open flower
C) Both open and closed flower
D) Neither open nor closed flower
Page No.-28, Easy
50. Flowers that do not open at all are called
A) Chasmogamous B) Polygamous
C) Cleistogamous D) Xenogamous
Page No.-28, Easy
